global.title: Second-life EV batteries approved for data center microgrid expansion
global.summary: Crusoe and Redwood Energy are scaling a microgrid in Sparks, Nevada, by using repurposed electric vehicle batteries and solar to supply additional modular data centers.

global.title: France lowers PV feed-in tariffs for systems up to 100 kW
global.summary: France’s energy regulator has reduced feed-in tariffs (FITs) for PV systems up to 100 kW for the April to July 2026 period, with lower rates across all segments and reduced compensation for surplus electricity.

global.title: Spain enables 50-plus renewable plants for real-time voltage control
global.summary: Spain’s grid operator Red Eléctrica and regulator CNMC have launched real-time voltage control services, allowing renewable energy installations to provide dynamic grid support under a new regulatory framework.

global.title: Global PV equipment market to reach 2.6 times current size by 2035
global.summary: A new study by Germany’s mechanical engineering association (VDMA) finds global capital expenditure on PV manufacturing equipment will grow to more than 2.5 times current levels by 2035, with European suppliers retaining competitive strengths for now despite structural challenges.

global.title: Sekisui-led consortium testing film-type perovskite solar for agrivolatics
global.summary: A Japanese consortium is piloting agrivoltaics using film-type perovskite solar cells over rice paddies to study energy generation alongside crop production. The three-year project will assess impacts on rice yield, land use, and emissions while testing the technology’s performance and commercial vi

global.title: How many solar projects have been backed by the UK government?
global.summary: Clean energy projects data reveals projects that have secured some kind of government backing since the last general election in July 2024. The largest projects are backed through contracts-for-difference allocation rounds, while rooftop support has been ramping up on schools and hospitals.

global.title: Saudi Arabia adds around 7.8 GW of solar in 2025
global.summary: Saudi Arabia had a record year for solar deployment last year, taking cumulative capacity past 12.4 GW. GlobalData is forecasting annual deployments to increase in the coming years but notes that they remain behind the pace required to meet the country's target of 130 GW of renewables by 2030, inste

global.title: South Korea targets 2,500 community solar cooperatives by 2030
global.summary: The South Korean government has launched a national drive to establish community-owned village solar cooperatives across the country, with more than 500 sites to be selected this year and KRW 550 billion ($366.4 million) in national funds earmarked for 2026.

global.title: Australian science agency trials AI robots for solar farms
global.summary: Australia’s national science agency says AI-driven robots could reshape solar maintenance across large-scale PV projects after successfully trialing the cutting-edge technology.

global.title: US installs record 18.9 GW of energy storage in 2025
global.summary: The United States added 18.9 GW of energy storage capacity in 2025, a 52% increase year on year, according to Wood Mackenzie.

global.title: Groups Challenge PSC’s Approval of Massive Georgia Power Gas Expansion
global.summary: ATLANTA, GA— Environmental organizations and faith communities filed an appeal today asking a Fulton County Superior Court to review the Georgia Public Service Commission’s December decision to approve Georgia Power’s proposal to add an additional 10 GW of energy generation to the grid at customers’

global.title: From Forklifts to the Future: Why My Switch to an MG S5 Just Makes Sense
global.summary: My friend Peter Tranter has recently made some big changes in life, moving from a medium sized property to a retirement village and also purchasing an MG S5 electric SUV. CleanTechnica readers were introduced to the MG S5 8 months ago here. I asked him if I could share his ... [continued] The post F

global.title: Europe Just 3 Years Behind China on Electric Vehicles Sales
global.summary: EVs are ‘super-lever’ to ending oil dependence; European oil imports set to rise to €300 billion in 2026, an €80 billion oil crisis premium. If Europe maintains its ambition on electric car uptake, it can close the gap with China before 2030 and radically reduce oil use in transport, new research fr

global.title: Sony & Honda Pull The Plug On Afeela
global.summary: Honda and Sony have canceled Afeela, the joint venture that was supposed to start selling new electric cars this year. The post Sony & Honda Pull The Plug On Afeela appeared first on CleanTechnica.

global.title: Donut Lab — Battery Specs Proven, But Not All Of Them
global.summary: The controversy surround the claim by Donut Lab that It has created a solid state battery continues to swirl. The post Donut Lab — Battery Specs Proven, But Not All Of Them appeared first on CleanTechnica.
